jokes.text: As in,'This game is stup-id.' - Hey, shut up, boy.
jokes.setup: Bart explains 'id' as part of a word 'stupid'
jokes.punchline: Homer's immediate angry response to being called stupid
jokes.explanation: Clever wordplay that reveals character dynamics - Bart's mischievousness and Homer's dim-witted defensiveness

jokes.text: We got one? - I think it's under the short leg of the couch.
jokes.setup: Homer asks if they have a dictionary
jokes.punchline: Marge reveals it's being used as furniture support
jokes.explanation: Perfect economy and class commentary - the intellectual tool reduced to home repair. Very Simpsons-esque observation

jokes.text: Kwyjibo. K-W-Y-J-I-B-O.
jokes.setup: Bart claims to have a high-scoring Scrabble word
jokes.punchline: The obviously made-up word 'Kwyjibo'
jokes.explanation: Iconic made-up word that became part of Simpsons lore. Perfect Bart move - creative cheating

jokes.text: A big, dumb, balding, North American ape with no chin. And a short temper.
jokes.setup: Bart is forced to define his made-up word
jokes.punchline: He describes Homer perfectly while pretending it's a real definition
jokes.explanation: Masterful setup/payoff - Bart insults his father while maintaining plausible deniability. Perfect character moment

jokes.text: Uh-oh. Kwyjibo on the loose.
jokes.setup: Homer chases Bart angrily
jokes.punchline: Bart uses his made-up word to describe the situation
jokes.explanation: Perfect callback that extends the joke - Bart commits to his creation even under threat

jokes.text: Student reports Bart for vandalism while Bart is actively spray-painting
jokes.setup: Martin tattles on anonymous vandalism
jokes.punchline: Bart is literally committing the crime as it's being reported
jokes.explanation: Good situational irony that establishes character dynamics

jokes.text: The preferred spelling of'wiener' is w-i-e-n-e-r... although 'e-i' is an acceptable ethnic variant.
jokes.setup: Skinner criticizes the vandalism's spelling
jokes.punchline: His pedantic lecture on proper wiener spelling
jokes.explanation: Brilliant Skinner moment - his academic obsession with correctness even for graffiti. Subverts expectation

jokes.text: you might say you caught him red-handed.
jokes.setup: Bart has red paint on his hands from graffiti
jokes.punchline: Skinner's literal/figurative pun on 'red-handed'
jokes.explanation: Straightforward pun, elevated by Skinner's pleased delivery. Not groundbreaking but serviceable

jokes.text: Eat my shorts.
jokes.setup: Martin gives Bart a pompous lecture
jokes.punchline: Bart's signature crude dismissal
jokes.explanation: Iconic Bart catchphrase - not original here but perfectly deployed character moment

jokes.text: These tests will have no effect on your grades. They merely determine your future social status and financial success. If any.
jokes.setup: Teacher reassures students about test importance
jokes.punchline: The devastating reality of what the test actually determines
jokes.explanation: Dark satire of standardized testing. The 'if any' addition is perfectly cruel and realistic

jokes.text: Oof!
jokes.setup: Complex math word problem overwhelms Bart
jokes.punchline: His simple grunt of defeat
jokes.explanation: Simple but effective reaction - shows Bart's overwhelm without words

jokes.text: May I go outside and read under a tree?
jokes.setup: Martin finishes the test quickly
jokes.punchline: His pretentious request to read under a tree
jokes.explanation: Perfect Martin - his academic pretension even extends to his leisure activities

jokes.text: That's exactly the kind of crapola that's lousing him up.
jokes.setup: Marge offers gentle parenting advice
jokes.punchline: Homer's crude dismissal of his wife's nurturing approach
jokes.explanation: Shows Homer/Marge dynamic - his blunt rejection of her maternal instincts

jokes.text: Hey, look at this. 'I am a wiener.' He sure is.
jokes.setup: They discover Bart's graffiti
jokes.punchline: Homer's matter-of-fact agreement with the insult
jokes.explanation: Homer's casual agreement with an insult about his son shows his parenting priorities

jokes.text: Yeah, it's a crummy system, but what are you gonna do?
jokes.setup: Skinner suggests Homer should pay for damage
jokes.punchline: Homer completely misses the point and agrees with the 'unfair' system
jokes.explanation: Perfect Homer obliviousness - he agrees with Skinner's sarcasm, missing that he's supposed to pay

jokes.text: What do we need a psychiatrist for? We know our kid is nuts.
jokes.setup: A psychiatrist arrives to discuss Bart
jokes.punchline: Homer's casual dismissal of his son's mental health
jokes.explanation: Dark but perfectly Homer - his matter-of-fact acceptance of his son being 'nuts'

jokes.text: Bart? This lunkhead? Impossible.
jokes.setup: Doctor reveals Bart is a genius
jokes.punchline: Homer's incredulous reaction with casual insult
jokes.explanation: Typical Homer - even good news about his son comes with an insult

jokes.text: 912! - Uh, no, you have it upside down. It's 216.
jokes.setup: Homer reads Bart's IQ score
jokes.punchline: Homer holds the paper upside down, misreading 216 as 912
jokes.explanation: Classic visual gag that reinforces Homer's intellectual limitations while delivering the plot point

jokes.text: Wow! It's like you're reading my mind, man.
jokes.setup: Doctor asks if Bart feels frustrated and bored in school
jokes.punchline: Bart's enthusiastic agreement, not realizing he's being evaluated
jokes.explanation: Shows Bart's obliviousness to the psychological evaluation, thinking the doctor actually understands him

jokes.text: Although, in some cases, it's a total mystery.
jokes.setup: Doctor explains genius is usually heredity and environment
jokes.punchline: He looks at Homer and adds the mystery caveat
jokes.explanation: Subtle dig at Homer - the doctor diplomatically suggests Bart's intelligence can't be explained by his parents

jokes.text: It could be one of these chemicals here that makes him so smart.
jokes.setup: Homer credits Bart's cereal for his intelligence
jokes.punchline: His belief that breakfast chemicals created genius
jokes.explanation: Homer's pseudo-scientific reasoning about processed food creating intelligence

jokes.text: Sort of a spare in case Bart's brain blows up.
jokes.setup: Homer suggests Lisa eat the same cereal
jokes.punchline: His mechanical view of genius as something that might literally explode
jokes.explanation: Brilliant Homer logic - treats intelligence like a mechanical device that might break, needs backup

jokes.text: I don't care what that stupid test says, Bart. You're a dimwit.
jokes.setup: Lisa observes the family celebrating Bart's genius
jokes.punchline: Her blunt assessment contradicting the test results
jokes.explanation: Lisa's superior intelligence showing - she sees through the false test results immediately

jokes.text: you may outsmart someone.
jokes.setup: Homer gives Bart an inspiring speech about achieving family dreams
jokes.punchline: The pathetically low bar of Simpson family ambition
jokes.explanation: Perfect encapsulation of Simpson family mediocrity - their highest aspiration is basic competence

jokes.text: Make your own rules. If you feel sleepy, take a nap. If you get bored, feel free to take out a book and start reading.
jokes.setup: Ms. Melon explains the progressive school's philosophy
jokes.punchline: The absurdly permissive educational approach
jokes.explanation: Satirizes progressive education - rules so permissive they're meaningless

jokes.text: O Memsahib, Bart. Rabbi has memo.
jokes.setup: Ethan is introduced as good with palindromes
jokes.punchline: His backwards greeting that sounds like nonsense
jokes.explanation: Clever palindrome work, though requiring explanation diminishes immediate impact

jokes.text: Trabing norm doog. - What's your problem?
jokes.setup: Sidney speaks in backwards phonetics
jokes.punchline: Bart's frustrated reaction to incomprehensible greeting
jokes.explanation: Shows Bart's growing alienation from the intellectual environment

jokes.text: I wouldn't get too attached. We're dissecting him next week.
jokes.setup: Bart shows affection for a cute hamster
jokes.punchline: Cecile's matter-of-fact announcement of its impending death
jokes.explanation: Dark humor contrasting child's affection with scientific detachment

jokes.text: Free will debate with complex philosophical discussion
jokes.setup: Elementary school classroom discussion
jokes.punchline: Children casually discussing advanced philosophical concepts
jokes.explanation: Sets up the contrast between gifted kids and Bart but runs long

jokes.text: Well... you're damned if you do... and you're damned if you don't.
jokes.setup: Bart is asked to provide an example of a paradox
jokes.punchline: His crude common saying amid sophisticated discussion
jokes.explanation: Shows Bart's intellectual limitations - he can only contribute street wisdom to academic discussion

jokes.text: I'll trade you the weight of a bowling ball on the eighth moon of Jupiter from my lunch for the weight of a feather on the second moon of Neptune from your lunch.
jokes.setup: Students are trading lunch items
jokes.punchline: The trade is phrased in impossibly complex scientific terms for simple food exchange
jokes.explanation: Brilliantly absurd over-intellectualization of basic childhood activity. Shows how these kids complicate everything unnecessarily.

jokes.text: A rather mediocre genius. Yes, not very bright at all.
jokes.setup: The genius kids evaluate Bart
jokes.punchline: Their contradictory assessment of a 'mediocre genius'
jokes.explanation: Perfect oxymoron highlighting the kids' pretentiousness and Bart's obvious mismatch

jokes.text: Os-os. - What? - That's backwards for so-so.
jokes.setup: Homer asks how school was
jokes.punchline: Bart mimics the backwards-speaking style from school
jokes.explanation: Shows Bart adopting the school's quirks while revealing his lukewarm experience

jokes.text: But I'm not a genius. Why should I suffer?
jokes.setup: Marge tells Homer he's going to the opera too
jokes.punchline: Homer's logical objection to cultural torture
jokes.explanation: Homer's perfect logic - why should non-geniuses endure high culture? Perfectly selfish reasoning

jokes.text: Geez. No beer. No opera dogs.
jokes.setup: Homer discovers opera venue amenities
jokes.punchline: His expectation of ballpark-style concessions at the opera
jokes.explanation: Homer applies working-class entertainment expectations to high culture

jokes.text: Toreador, oh, don't spit on the floor, Please use the cuspidor, That's what it's for
jokes.setup: Bart listens to opera performance
jokes.punchline: His crude parody of Carmen with bathroom humor
jokes.explanation: Classic Bart - reduces high art to crude humor, but shows creativity in the parody

jokes.text: No way a bull's gonna miss a target that big, man.
jokes.setup: Bart watches large opera singer as bullfighter
jokes.punchline: His logical assessment of the fat lady as unmissable target
jokes.explanation: Bart's practical observation about opera staging - reduces drama to physics

jokes.text: It ain't over till the fat lady sings. Is that one fat enough for you, son?
jokes.setup: Homer explains the famous expression
jokes.punchline: Perfect visual timing with actual fat lady singing opera
jokes.explanation: Perfect setup/payoff using cultural reference - literal fat lady singing at the exact right moment

jokes.text: Har-dee-har-har. Get it?
jokes.setup: Math equation that sounds like laughter when read aloud
jokes.punchline: Kid's proud explanation of his mathematical pun
jokes.explanation: Clever wordplay but the need for explanation kills the comedy - shows pretentious nature of genius kids

jokes.text: Get lost, Poindexter. Yeah, beat it, Professor. Why don't you go build a rocket ship, brainiac?
jokes.setup: Bart's old friends see him
jokes.punchline: They reject him with nerd-related insults
jokes.explanation: Shows Bart's social isolation - his reputation has changed his friendships

jokes.text: 'Course I do.
jokes.setup: Teacher asks if Bart knows what happens mixing acids and bases
jokes.punchline: His confident assertion followed by immediate explosion
jokes.explanation: Perfect setup for physical comedy - Bart's overconfidence immediately contradicted by results

jokes.text: it doesn't take a Bart Simpson to figure out that something's wrong.
jokes.setup: Teacher discusses Bart's problems
jokes.punchline: Uses 'Bart Simpson' as synonym for genius in ironic context
jokes.explanation: Brilliant wordplay using common expression with character's name - meta humor that works on multiple levels

jokes.text: Like Jane Goodall and the chimps.
jokes.setup: Bart wants to study 'regular dumb kids' undercover
jokes.punchline: Teacher's comparison to studying primates
jokes.explanation: Perfect academic reference that reveals the condescending attitude toward regular students

jokes.text: Bart's confession note: 'I am a regular dumb kid. I cheated on my intelligence test.'
jokes.setup: Bart writing academic proposal
jokes.punchline: Simple confession instead of complex academic writing
jokes.explanation: Perfect Bart moment - cutting through academic pretense with simple honesty

jokes.text: I bet Einstein turned himself all sorts of colors... before he invented the lightbulb.
jokes.setup: Homer tries to encourage Bart after chemistry accident
jokes.punchline: His completely wrong historical facts about Einstein
jokes.explanation: Perfect Homer - attempts wisdom but reveals complete ignorance of basic history and science

jokes.text: And I think if something can bring us that close... it can't possibly be bad.
jokes.setup: Bart confesses his deception but explains the positive family bonding
jokes.punchline: His philosophical justification for lying
jokes.explanation: Shows Bart's manipulative intelligence - using genuine sentiment to justify deception

jokes.text: Lisa: 'I think Bart's stupid again, Mom.' Marge: 'Oh, well.'
jokes.setup: Homer chasing Bart through the house
jokes.punchline: Family's casual acceptance of return to normalcy
jokes.explanation: Perfect family dynamic showing resigned acceptance of Bart's true nature

jokes.text: I think Bart's stupid again, Mom. Oh, well.
jokes.setup: Sounds of Homer chasing Bart angrily
jokes.punchline: Lisa and Marge's casual acceptance of the situation
jokes.explanation: Perfect family dysfunction - they treat major revelations as mundane. Lisa's matter-of-fact delivery is brilliant

jokes.text: you think I'm dumb enough to fall for that? I'm insulted.
jokes.setup: Homer tries to lure Bart out with affection
jokes.punchline: Bart's indignant response about being considered dumb while hiding from consequences
jokes.explanation: Perfect irony - Bart objects to being called dumb immediately after proving his intelligence through deception
